{"id":"pm-609.3","title":"Define repository protocol interfaces in core/protocols.py","description":"Create core/protocols.py with typing.Protocol classes for all repositories. Each protocol defines the public API contract that SQL (and potentially other) implementations must satisfy. All methods return typed domain objects, not raw dicts. Protocols: TaskRepository, ProjectRepository, RaidRepository, ActionRepository, MessageRepository, JournalRepository.","acceptance_criteria":"1. core/protocols.py exists with 6 Protocol classes.
2. TaskRepository: list_all() -> list[Task], get(), update_status(), update_blocking().
3. ProjectRepository: read() -> Project, update_health(), update_milestone().
4. RaidRepository: list_all() -> list[RaidItem], add(item: RaidItem) -> int, update().
5. ActionRepository: list_all() -> list[Action], add(action: Action) -> int, update_status().
6. MessageRepository: send(), list_inbox() -> list[Message], list_outbox() -> list[Message].
7. JournalRepository: read_last(), write(), list_all().
8. No concrete implementations in this file.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:32:43Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:32:43Z"}

new_value: Created core/protocols.py with 6 Protocol classes (TaskRepository, ProjectRepository, RaidRepository, ActionRepository, MessageRepository, JournalRepository) returning typed domain objects

{"id":"pm-609.6","title":"Implement SQLite-backed repositories in core/db/repositories.py","description":"Create core/db/repositories.py with concrete repository implementations that satisfy the Protocol interfaces. Classes: SqliteTaskRepository, SqliteProjectRepository, SqliteRaidRepository, SqliteActionRepository, SqliteMessageRepository — each accepts a Session in constructor. Also FileJournalRepository that wraps existing file-based journal logic behind JournalRepository protocol, accepting journal_dir: Path as constructor arg. Each SQL repo maps between ORM models and domain dataclasses via _to_domain() helpers. Commits within each write method (immediate persistence).","acceptance_criteria":"1. 5 SQL repo classes + 1 file-based repo class, all conforming to their Protocol.
2. Each SQL repo accepts Session, queries ORM models, returns domain dataclasses.
3. SqliteTaskRepository.list_all() returns list[Task] with proper date deserialisation.
4. SqliteProjectRepository.read() returns Project with list[Phase] and list[Milestone].
5. SqliteRaidRepository.list_all() returns list[RaidItem].
6. SqliteActionRepository.list_all() returns list[Action].
7. SqliteMessageRepository replaces Mailbox: send() inserts outbound, list_inbox()/list_outbox() filter by direction.
8. FileJournalRepository wraps existing file logic with configurable journal_dir.
9. All repos commit immediately on writes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:33:22Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:33:22Z"}

{"id":"pm-owr.1","title":"Detect and flag overdue tasks each agent run","description":"Add overdue detection to the PM agent daily loop. On each run, compare task due_date against REFERENCE_DATE. Tasks past due and not complete should be flagged as overdue. The agent should log overdue tasks in the journal, change reminder tone (urgent vs standard), and not treat them as normal pending tasks. This replaces the current behaviour where overdue tasks get the same reminder as upcoming ones. Backlog item #3.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agent identifies tasks where due_date < REFERENCE_DATE and status != complete.
2. Overdue tasks are logged distinctly in the journal (separate section or clear labelling).
3. Reminders for overdue tasks use urgent tone, distinct from standard reminders.
4. Overdue count is reflected in the project health assessment.
5. No false positives — tasks completed on or before due_date are not flagged.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:39Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:39Z"}

new_value: Added fetch_overdue_tasks tool and updated PM prompt with urgent vs standard reminder tone for overdue tasks

old_value:
{"id":"pm-owr.2","title":"Enforce task dependency logic in agent decisions","description":"The depends_on field already exists on Task but the agent doesn't enforce it. Add logic so the agent: (1) does not send reminders for tasks whose upstream dependencies are incomplete, (2) flags these as 'blocked by dependency' in the journal, (3) considers dependency chains when assessing milestone risk. Backlog item #5.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agent skips reminders for tasks where any depends_on task is not complete.
2. Blocked-by-dependency tasks are noted in the journal with which task blocks them.
3. Milestone risk assessment considers dependency chain completion.
4. When a dependency completes, the downstream task becomes eligible for reminders on the next run.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z"}

new_value: Added fetch_dependency_blocked_tasks tool and updated prompt to skip reminders for dependency-blocked tasks, flag them in journal, and consider dependency chains for milestone risk

old_value:
{"id":"pm-owr.3","title":"Add priority field to Task model and agent triage","description":"Add a priority field (high/medium/low) to the Task dataclass, ORM model, and SQL schema. Update the agent to use priority for triage: high-priority overdue tasks get escalated sooner, reminder tone is tailored by priority, and the journal groups tasks by priority. Backlog item #6.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Task dataclass has priority: Optional[Literal['high', 'medium', 'low']] field.
2. ORM model and SQL table include priority column.
3. Agent tailors reminder urgency based on priority (high = more urgent tone).
4. Journal task review groups or sorts by priority.
5. Existing tasks without priority default to medium.
6. Demo data includes tasks with varied priorities.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z"}

new_value: Added priority field to Task model, ORM, seed data, web UI, reporter context, and agent prompt with priority-based triage

old_value:
{"id":"pm-owr.4","title":"Send advance warning reminders before task due dates","description":"Remind task owners 1-2 days before a task is due, not just on the day or after. The warning lead time should be configurable (default: 2 days). The agent should check upcoming due dates each run and send a friendly heads-up to owners whose tasks are approaching deadline. Backlog item #7.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agent sends advance reminders for tasks due within N days (default N=2).
2. Advance reminders are distinct in tone from day-of and overdue reminders.
3. Warning lead time is configurable (via config or constant).
4. No duplicate advance reminders — only sent once per task per warning window.
5. Advance reminders are logged in the journal.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:40Z"}

new_value: Added fetch_upcoming_due_tasks tool and prompt updates for advance warning reminders before task due dates

old_value:
{"id":"pm-593.1","title":"Tag outbox messages with task_id for per-task chaser tracking","description":"Add a task_id field to the Message model and SQL schema. When the agent sends a reminder for a specific task, tag the message with that task's ID. Update the chaser frequency check to be per-task rather than per-person — currently 'last message sent to Bob' is too coarse when Bob owns multiple tasks. Backlog item #4.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Message dataclass and ORM model include optional task_id field.
2. send_message tool accepts optional task_id parameter.
3. Agent tags reminders with the relevant task_id when sending.
4. Chaser deduplication checks last message per (owner, task_id) pair, not just per owner.
5. Existing messages without task_id continue to work (field is nullable).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z"}

new_value: Added task_id field to Message model, ORM, repository, service, tool, and prompt for per-task chaser tracking

old_value:
{"id":"pm-593.2","title":"Add escalation logic for persistently overdue tasks","description":"If a task is N days overdue with no completion or response, send an escalation message to a separate recipient (e.g. project sponsor or manager). N should be configurable (default: 3 days). Escalation should reference the task, owner, and how many days overdue. Only escalate once per task unless the situation worsens. Backlog item #8.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Escalation triggers after N days overdue with no completion (default N=3).
2. Escalation message sent to a configurable escalation recipient.
3. Message references specific task, owner, days overdue.
4. Escalation is logged in the journal.
5. Re-escalation only occurs if overdue period extends significantly (e.g. doubles).
6. Escalation threshold N is configurable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z"}

new_value: Added escalation logic: fetch_escalation_candidates tool (configurable threshold, default 3 days, checks for prior escalations) and prompt updates for sponsor-level escalation with re-escalation guards

old_value:
{"id":"pm-593.3","title":"Parse inbox messages for structured intent","description":"Extract structured intent from inbox messages using LLM parsing: completion confirmation, blocker report, date change request, general update, etc. Use the parsed intent to drive agent decisions rather than treating all replies as raw text. For example, a completion confirmation should trigger task status update; a blocker report should trigger task blocking and RAID item creation. Backlog item #9.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inbox messages are parsed into intent categories: completion, blocker, date_change, general.
2. Completion intent triggers automatic task status update to complete.
3. Blocker intent triggers task blocking and optionally creates a RAID issue.
4. Date change intent is logged for PM review (or auto-updates if confidence is high).
5. Parsed intent is logged in the journal alongside the raw message.
6. Parsing uses the LLM — no brittle regex matching.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:42Z"}

new_value: Implemented LLM-based inbox intent parsing tool and updated prompt to use structured intents

old_value:
{"id":"pm-atk.1","title":"Externalise thresholds into a config file","description":"Move hardcoded behaviour thresholds into a data/config.json (or similar) file: chaser frequency (how often to re-remind), advance warning days, escalation threshold (days overdue before escalating), escalation recipient. Agent reads config at startup. Changing behaviour should require only editing the config file, not code. Backlog item #12.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Config file exists at a known path (e.g. data/config.json).
2. Contains: chaser_frequency_days, advance_warning_days, escalation_threshold_days, escalation_recipient.
3. Agent loads config at startup and uses values throughout the daily loop.
4. Sensible defaults if config file is missing.
5. No code changes needed to tune thresholds.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z"}

new_value: Externalised thresholds (chaser_frequency_days, advance_warning_days, escalation_threshold_days, re_escalation_gap_days) into data/config.json with a config loader module at core/config.py

old_value:
{"id":"pm-atk.2","title":"Add idempotency guard for same-day agent reruns","description":"Prevent double-sends and duplicate journal entries if the agent runs more than once in a day (e.g. after a crash and restart). Before acting, check whether today's loop has already completed by looking for an existing journal entry or a run-completion marker. If already run, skip or warn rather than duplicating messages and entries. Backlog item #13.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agent detects if today's daily loop has already completed.
2. On rerun, duplicate messages are not sent.
3. On rerun, duplicate journal sections are not appended.
4. Detection mechanism is reliable (e.g. completion marker in DB or journal).
5. Agent can be forced to rerun if needed (e.g. --force flag).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z"}

new_value: Added idempotency guard: checks for existing journal entry before running daily loop

{"id":"pm-8ey.1","title":"Generate weekly summary report across journals","description":"Generate a weekly markdown summary covering all journals from the past 7 days: tasks completed, tasks now overdue, tasks at risk, key decisions made, and open blockers. Output to data/reports/YYYY-Www.md. Can run as a standalone command or be triggered at the end of a week's final agent run. Backlog item #10.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Weekly report generated at data/reports/YYYY-Www.md.
2. Report covers tasks completed, overdue, at risk during the week.
3. Key decisions and blockers summarised.
4. Can be run via standalone CLI command.
5. Report format is consistent with daily status reports.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z"}

new_value: Implemented weekly summary report generator with journal range reading

{"id":"pm-8ey.2","title":"Write machine-readable status snapshot on each agent run","description":"Write a data/status.json file on each agent run containing a snapshot of all task states, project health, and key metrics. Enables external tools, CI pipelines, and dashboards to consume current state without parsing journals or querying the DB directly. Partially superseded by the web UI (Epic 3) but remains useful for automation consumers. Backlog item #11.","acceptance_criteria":"1. data/status.json written on each agent run.
2. Contains: all task statuses, project RAG, milestone statuses, overdue count, blocked count.
3. File is valid JSON and parseable by external tools.
4. Snapshot reflects state after the agent's daily loop completes.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"paul.mclear@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z","created_by":"Paul McLear","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:45:44Z"}
